_comment: Canonical scoring ruleset for MacVitals. The web app imports this directly. The bash analyzer (macvitals-analyze.sh) loads it at runtime when present and falls back to identical built-in defaults when run standalone. Keep messages and tunables here as the single source of truth. See shared/README.md.

critical.label: DO NOT BUY YET — serious issues must be resolved first

warn.label: PROCEED WITH CAUTION — some concerns to check or negotiate on

messages.smart.ok: SSD self-check (SMART) reports "Verified" — the drive is not reporting failures.
messages.smart.bad: SSD SMART status is "{smart}" (should be "Verified"). This points to a failing or degraded drive.
messages.smart.unknown: SSD SMART status could not be read — verify the drive health manually before buying.
messages.trim.ok: SSD TRIM is enabled (normal, keeps the SSD healthy).
messages.activationLock.ok: Activation Lock is OFF — you will be able to set the Mac up as your own.
messages.activationLock.on: Activation Lock is ON. Until the seller removes it from their Apple ID, this Mac is effectively unusable by you.
messages.activationLock.unknown: Activation Lock status was not reported — confirm it is OFF before paying (System Settings > General > About, or ask the seller to sign out of iCloud in front of you).
messages.mdm.managed: The Mac is still enrolled in a company's device-management (MDM) system. A managed Mac can be remotely locked, wiped, or restricted by the previous owner's IT. It MUST be released from management before you buy.
messages.profiles.present: {n} configuration profile(s) are installed. On a properly wiped machine there should be none — these are leftovers from the previous (likely corporate) owner. Ask for a clean macOS reinstall.
messages.profiles.none: No leftover configuration profiles — consistent with a clean machine.
messages.sip.ok: System Integrity Protection (macOS's core self-protection) is enabled — normal and good.
messages.sip.off: System Integrity Protection appears disabled — unusual; ask why it was turned off.
messages.filevault.on: FileVault disk encryption is currently ON. Make sure the seller either turns it off or does a full erase + reinstall of macOS at handover — otherwise you could be locked out of the disk.
messages.battery.good: Battery at {cap}% of original capacity — healthy.
messages.battery.fine: Battery at {cap}% capacity — mild wear, still fine for daily use.
messages.battery.worn: Battery at {cap}% capacity — noticeable wear. Replaceable later at an Apple service centre if it bothers you.
messages.battery.bad: Battery at {cap}% capacity — significantly worn. Budget for a replacement (this is a known, planned cost).
messages.panics.present: {n} kernel-panic report(s) found on disk. Occasional panics can be software; repeated ones can indicate failing hardware. Check system.txt and ask the seller about crashes.
messages.panics.none: No kernel-panic crash reports found — a good stability sign.
messages.thermal.throttled: CPU was thermally throttled (speed limit {limit}%) at the moment of capture. If it stays below 100% when idle/cool, suspect a thermal problem (dried paste, dust, or fan).
messages.thermal.ok: CPU was running at full speed (no thermal throttling) at capture time.

nextSteps: Ask the seller to sign out of iCloud and erase the Mac (System Settings > General > Transfer or Reset > Erase All Content and Settings) IN FRONT OF YOU. This clears Activation Lock, device management, profiles and FileVault in one step.
Physically test every port with a real device/charger, and test the camera, speakers, microphone and keyboard.
Run Apple's built-in hardware test (see diagnostics.txt in the report folder) and note the reference code — ADP000 means no issues.
nextStepsCriticalExtra: Because DEAL-BREAKERS were found, do not pay until they are fixed and you re-run this tool to confirm they are gone.
